choices: Learning about the world around us
A school subject with no uses
mcq.explain: Science is the study of the world around us — how things work, from tiny cells to huge stars.

mcq.q: Which of these is a science process skill?
mcq.explain: Scientists observe using their five senses to gather information.

mcq.q: What is the first step of the scientific method?
mcq.explain: Scientists begin by making an observation — noticing something interesting.

choices: A wild guess with no thinking
A careful guess based on what you know
mcq.explain: A prediction is an educated guess based on prior knowledge — also called a hypothesis.

mcq.q: Which tool would you use to measure temperature?
mcq.explain: A thermometer measures temperature in degrees Celsius or Fahrenheit.

mcq.q: Why do scientists record their results?
choices: To share and repeat their work
mcq.explain: Recording allows scientists to share findings and for others to repeat the experiment.

mcq.q: What does an experiment help a scientist to do?
mcq.explain: An experiment is a fair test designed to check whether a prediction is correct.

mcq.q: Which sense would help you observe the colour of a leaf?
mcq.explain: Colour is observed using sight — your eyes.

mcq.q: Which tool measures length in centimetres?
mcq.explain: A ruler is marked in centimetres and millimetres for measuring length.

mcq.q: A balance is a tool used to measure…
mcq.explain: A balance compares masses, telling you how heavy an object is.

mcq.q: What unit do scientists usually use for liquid volume?
mcq.explain: Volume of liquid is measured in millilitres (mL) or litres (L).

choices: Changing one thing and keeping others the same
mcq.explain: In a fair test, only one variable is changed so you know what caused the result.

choices: An educated guess to be tested
mcq.explain: A hypothesis is a careful, testable prediction made before an experiment.
A biologist studies plants, animals and other living things.

mcq.q: Why do we wear safety goggles in the lab?
mcq.explain: Goggles protect your eyes from splashes and flying bits.

mcq.q: Which step comes after 'do the experiment'?
mcq.explain: After doing the experiment, scientists record the results so they can study them.

choices: To check the results are reliable
mcq.explain: Repeating an experiment shows whether the result happens every time, not just by luck.

mcq.q: A diagram of a result helps a scientist to…
mcq.explain: Charts and diagrams make it easy to see and share patterns in the data.
Classifying is sorting things into groups by what they have in common.

mcq.q: If a prediction is wrong, a scientist should…
choices: Learn from it and try again
mcq.explain: Wrong predictions are useful — they teach scientists something new and lead to better tests.

flashcards.back: Using the five senses to gather information
A careful prediction about what might happen
A fair test of a prediction
The step-by-step way scientists answer questions
Grouping things by similarities and differences
Finding the exact size, amount, or quantity using tools
Sharing what you found with others

mcq.q: Which of these is NOT a characteristic of living things?
mcq.explain: Living things grow, reproduce, breathe, need food and water — they are not made of metal.

mcq.q: Which group does a Jamaican ackee tree belong to?
mcq.explain: Ackee is the national fruit of Jamaica and is a plant.

mcq.q: What do all living things need to survive?
mcq.explain: All living things need food, water, air, and a suitable environment.
A mongoose is a mammal — it is warm-blooded, has fur, and feeds milk to its young.

mcq.q: Living things that make their own food are called…
mcq.explain: Plants are producers — they make their own food through photosynthesis.
Reptiles like crocodiles are cold-blooded — their body temperature changes with the environment.
An iguana grows, breathes and reproduces, so it is alive.

mcq.q: What do we call a baby plant growing from a seed?
mcq.explain: A young plant pushing out of a seed is called a sprout or seedling.

choices: Making more of the same kind
mcq.explain: Reproduction is how living things make new young of their own kind.
Sand does not grow, breathe or reproduce, so it is non-living.
Animals breathe in oxygen from the air to stay alive.

mcq.q: Which group does a fish belong to?
mcq.explain: Fish live in water and breathe through gills — they are their own group.

mcq.q: A mammal is an animal that…
choices: Has fur or hair and feeds milk to its young
mcq.explain: Mammals have hair or fur and feed their babies on milk.

mcq.q: Which Jamaican animal is a bird?
mcq.explain: The doctor bird is Jamaica's national bird, a hummingbird.

mcq.q: Plants belong to which group of living things?
mcq.explain: Plants are their own kingdom of living things — they make their own food.

mcq.q: Which living thing is too small to see without a microscope?
mcq.explain: Bacteria are tiny one-celled living things, only visible through a microscope.

mcq.q: Animals that eat only plants are called…
mcq.explain: Herbivores feed only on plants — like cows and goats.

mcq.q: Why do living things need water?
mcq.explain: Water is needed for the chemical reactions inside the cells of every living thing.

mcq.q: Which is a sign that something is alive?
mcq.explain: Growth is one of the main signs of life.

mcq.q: A frog starts life as a…
mcq.explain: Frogs hatch from eggs as tadpoles, then change into frogs — that's metamorphosis.

flashcards.back: Something that grows, reproduces, and needs food, water, and air
Something that does not grow or reproduce (e.g., a rock)
Warm-blooded animal with fur that feeds its babies milk
Cold-blooded animal with scales (e.g., lizard, crocodile)
A living thing that makes its own food (plants)
A living thing that eats other living things
The place where a plant or animal lives
A special feature that helps a living thing survive

mcq.q: Which part of the plant makes food?
mcq.explain: Leaves make food using sunlight in a process called photosynthesis.

mcq.q: What gas do plants take in to make food?
mcq.explain: Plants take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen during photosynthesis.

choices: Holding the plant in soil and soaking up water
mcq.explain: Roots anchor the plant and absorb water and minerals from the soil.
A lion is a carnivore — it eats other animals.

mcq.q: A plant with flowers is called a…
mcq.explain: Flowering plants produce flowers, which then make seeds.
Bees carry pollen from flower to flower, helping plants make seeds.

mcq.q: Which part of a plant takes in water from the soil?
mcq.explain: Roots soak up water and minerals from the soil.

mcq.q: The stem of a plant carries…
choices: Water and food up and down the plant
mcq.explain: The stem is like a pipe, moving water up and food down.

mcq.q: What process do plants use to make food?
mcq.explain: Photosynthesis uses sunlight, water and carbon dioxide to make food in the leaves.
A cow eats only plants, so it is a herbivore.

mcq.q: An omnivore is an animal that eats…
mcq.explain: Omnivores like humans eat both plants and animals.

mcq.q: Which of these animals is a reptile?
mcq.explain: Iguanas are reptiles — cold-blooded animals with scales.

mcq.q: What do flowers help plants to do?
mcq.explain: Flowers contain the parts that make seeds for new plants.

mcq.q: Animals that eat only meat are called…
mcq.explain: Carnivores eat only other animals.

mcq.q: Which is the food-making part of a leaf?
mcq.explain: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in leaves that traps sunlight to make food.
Plants produce their own food using sunlight, so they are called producers.

mcq.q: Which part of the plant grows into a fruit?
mcq.explain: After a flower is pollinated, parts of it grow into the fruit.

mcq.q: A food chain usually starts with a…
mcq.explain: Plants are at the start of a food chain because they make their own food.
Hens lay eggs from which chicks hatch.

choices: To make food in their leaves
To grow flowers in the dark
mcq.explain: Sunlight is the energy plants use during photosynthesis to make food.

flashcards.back: How plants make food using sunlight, water, and air
Moving pollen from one flower to another
When a seed starts to grow into a new plant
An animal that eats only plants
An animal that eats other animals
An animal that eats both plants and animals
The male part of a flower that makes pollen
The female part of a flower that receives pollen

mcq.explain: Eyes allow us to see light and colours.
Taste buds on the tongue detect sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami.

mcq.q: Which sense organ is used to smell?
mcq.explain: The nose contains special cells that detect smells in the air.

mcq.q: How many main sense organs do humans have?
mcq.explain: We have 5 main sense organs: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and skin.

mcq.q: The skin is the sense organ for…
mcq.explain: The skin has nerves that let us feel touch, heat, cold, and pain.

mcq.q: Why should you not look directly at the sun?
choices: It is too bright and can damage your eyes
mcq.explain: The sun's light is so bright it can permanently damage the eyes.

mcq.q: Which sense organ is the ear?
mcq.explain: Ears collect sound waves so we can hear.

mcq.q: Which part of the eye lets light enter?
mcq.explain: The pupil is the dark hole in the centre of the eye that lets light in.

mcq.q: You can tell a flower smells sweet using your…
mcq.explain: The nose is the sense organ for smell.

mcq.q: Tiny bumps on the tongue that taste food are called…
mcq.explain: Taste buds on your tongue help you sense sweet, sour, salty and bitter flavours.

mcq.q: Which sense helps you feel that ice is cold?
mcq.explain: Touch through your skin tells you about temperature and texture.

mcq.q: Loud noises can damage which sense organ?
mcq.explain: Very loud sounds can hurt the delicate parts inside the ears.

mcq.q: Which sense would warn you that food is burning?
mcq.explain: Smelling smoke quickly warns you that food is burning.

mcq.q: To keep your eyes healthy you should…
mcq.explain: Reading in good light keeps your eyes from getting tired.

mcq.q: Which sense lets you tell sugar from salt?
mcq.explain: You taste sugar as sweet and salt as salty using your tongue.

mcq.q: Brushing your teeth helps which sense organ?
mcq.explain: Brushing keeps the tongue and mouth healthy so you can taste well.

mcq.q: Which sense is most important for a baker checking baked goods?
mcq.explain: Smell helps a baker know when something is ready or burning.

mcq.q: Which body part protects the brain?
mcq.explain: The hard skull surrounds and protects the brain.

mcq.q: If your skin feels something hot, you should…
mcq.explain: Pulling away from a hot object stops you from getting burned.

mcq.q: Which sense helps a blind person read braille?
mcq.explain: Braille uses raised dots that are read by touch with the fingertips.

flashcards.back: Sense organ for hearing and balance
The coloured part of the eye
The thin part of the ear that vibrates
Tiny bumps on the tongue that sense flavour

mcq.explain: Wood comes from trees — it is natural. Plastic, nylon, and polyester are man-made.

mcq.q: A material that light can pass through is called…
mcq.explain: Transparent materials (like clear glass) let light pass through clearly.

mcq.q: Which property is metal well known for?
mcq.explain: Most metals conduct (carry) electricity and heat well.

mcq.q: Which state of matter takes the shape of its container?
mcq.explain: Liquids flow and take the shape of whatever container they are in.

mcq.q: Why is glass used for windows?
choices: It is transparent so light can pass through
mcq.explain: Glass is transparent, allowing sunlight into the room.

mcq.q: Which is an example of a gas?
mcq.explain: Air is a gas — it spreads out to fill any space.
Wood is a natural material taken from trees.
Plastic is made by people in factories from chemicals.

mcq.q: A material that does not let light through is called…
mcq.explain: Opaque materials block all light, like a wall or wooden door.

mcq.q: Which state of matter has a fixed shape?
mcq.explain: Solids keep their shape unless something changes them.

mcq.q: Which material is best for making a raincoat?
mcq.explain: Plastic is waterproof, so rain runs off without soaking through.

mcq.q: Which is a property of metal?
choices: It is soft like a sponge
mcq.explain: Most metals carry heat well, which is why pots are made from metal.

mcq.q: Why is rubber used to make tyres?
choices: It bends and grips the road
mcq.explain: Rubber is flexible and gives good grip, perfect for tyres.

mcq.q: Which material is attracted by a magnet?
mcq.explain: Iron and steel are magnetic — magnets pull them.

choices: Takes the shape of its container
mcq.explain: Liquids flow and fill the bottom of whatever holds them.
Cotton fibres come from the cotton plant and are spun into thread.

mcq.q: Which material is best for keeping coffee hot?
mcq.explain: Foam is an insulator — it slows the heat from escaping.

mcq.q: What happens to chocolate when it gets very hot?
mcq.explain: Heat changes chocolate from a solid into a liquid — this is melting.

mcq.q: Which is an example of a solid?
mcq.explain: A brick has a fixed shape, so it is a solid.

mcq.q: Which property makes glass useful for windows?
mcq.explain: Glass is transparent — light passes through it so you can see outside.

flashcards.back: Has fixed shape and volume (e.g., rock)
Takes the shape of its container, fixed volume (e.g., water)
Spreads out to fill any space (e.g., air)
Some light passes through but you cannot see clearly

mcq.q: Which is the largest part of the Earth's surface?
mcq.explain: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered with water.

mcq.q: When water turns into a gas, we call it…
mcq.explain: Evaporation happens when water is heated and turns into water vapour (gas).
About 78% of the air is nitrogen; about 21% is oxygen.

mcq.q: The water cycle includes which steps?
mcq.explain: Water evaporates, condenses into clouds, then falls as precipitation (rain).
The Rio Grande in Portland is famous for rafting.
All living things need clean water to survive.

mcq.q: Which gas in the air do living things breathe in?
mcq.explain: Living things breathe in oxygen to release energy from food.

mcq.q: Water that we can drink is called…
mcq.explain: Potable water is clean and safe to drink.

mcq.q: Which is a state of water?
mcq.explain: Water exists as solid ice, liquid water, and steam (gas).

mcq.q: What do we call rain, snow, and hail together?
mcq.explain: Precipitation is any form of water that falls from the sky.

mcq.q: When water vapour cools and turns back into a liquid, this is called…
mcq.explain: Condensation is when gas cools to a liquid, like dew on a glass.

mcq.q: What temperature does pure water freeze at?
mcq.explain: Pure water freezes into ice at 0°C.

mcq.q: Which body of water is salty?
mcq.explain: Seas and oceans are salty because of the dissolved minerals.
Wind is just air on the move.

mcq.q: Plants take in which gas during photosynthesis?
mcq.explain: Plants use carbon dioxide together with water and sunlight to make food.

mcq.q: To save water at home, you should…
mcq.explain: Shorter showers use much less water than long ones.

mcq.q: What do we call dirty water that has things harmful to health?
mcq.explain: Polluted water has been mixed with harmful substances.

mcq.q: Which is the lightest of these?
mcq.explain: Air is much lighter than the other things, but it still has weight.
Wind pushes against a kite and lifts it into the air.

mcq.q: Boiling water at sea level happens at…
mcq.explain: At sea level, water boils at 100°C and changes to steam.

flashcards.back: Water changes from liquid to gas
Water vapour cools and becomes liquid
Water falling from clouds as rain, sleet, or snow
The never-ending journey of water on Earth
The blanket of air surrounding Earth
The gas in air that we breathe in
The gas we breathe out; plants use it to make food
Harmful things added to air, water, or land

mcq.q: Which organ pumps blood around the body?
mcq.explain: The heart is a muscle that pumps blood through blood vessels.
Lungs take in oxygen and push out carbon dioxide.

mcq.q: How many bones does the adult human body have?
mcq.explain: Adults have 206 bones, forming the skeleton.
Muscles contract and pull bones so that we can move.

mcq.q: Which food group gives us the most energy?
mcq.explain: Carbohydrates like yam, rice, and bread give our bodies energy.

choices: Eat balanced meals, exercise and sleep well
mcq.explain: Balanced meals, exercise, rest, and clean water are key to health.

mcq.q: Which body system breaks down food?
mcq.explain: The digestive system breaks food into small pieces the body can use.

mcq.q: Which organ controls your thoughts and movements?
mcq.explain: The brain is the control centre of the body.

mcq.q: Blood travels around the body inside…
mcq.explain: Blood flows in tubes called blood vessels — arteries and veins.

mcq.q: Which food is a good source of protein?
mcq.explain: Fish, chicken, beans and eggs are all rich in protein for body building.

mcq.q: Why is exercise good for you?
choices: It keeps your heart and muscles strong
mcq.explain: Regular exercise builds strong muscles and a healthy heart.

mcq.q: Which part of your body lets you bend your arm?
mcq.explain: Joints, like the elbow, are places where bones meet and move.

mcq.q: What gas do your lungs breathe out?
mcq.explain: You breathe out carbon dioxide, a waste gas your body makes.

mcq.q: Why should you wash your hands before eating?
mcq.explain: Washing removes germs that could make you sick.

mcq.q: Which food group helps you grow strong bones?
mcq.explain: Dairy foods like milk are rich in calcium for strong bones.

mcq.q: Which organ removes waste from the blood?
mcq.explain: Kidneys filter blood and make urine, which carries waste out of the body.

mcq.q: How many times a day should most children brush their teeth?
mcq.explain: Brushing twice a day, morning and night, keeps teeth clean and strong.

choices: Helps the body rest and grow
mcq.explain: During sleep your body repairs itself and your brain stores what you learned.

mcq.q: Which part of the body sends messages from the brain?
mcq.explain: Nerves are like wires that carry signals between the brain and the body.

mcq.q: Which is the largest organ of the body?
mcq.explain: The skin covers your whole body and is your largest organ.

flashcards.back: Take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide
All 206 bones that support the body
Breaks down food into smaller parts

mcq.q: A push or a pull is called a…
mcq.explain: A force is any push or pull acting on an object.

mcq.q: What force pulls things toward the Earth?
mcq.explain: Gravity pulls objects with mass toward Earth's centre.
Friction is a force that slows movement between two surfaces.
Electricity powers appliances like lights, TVs, and fans.

mcq.q: The sun is a source of…
mcq.explain: The sun provides both light and heat energy to Earth.
Batteries store chemical energy, which is changed into electrical energy.

mcq.q: Which force keeps a ball rolling down a hill?
mcq.explain: Gravity pulls the ball down the slope of the hill.
Rough, dry surfaces grip more, so they have more friction.

mcq.q: Energy from the sun is called…
mcq.explain: Solar energy is the light and heat that come from the Sun.

mcq.q: A magnet attracts things made of…
mcq.explain: Magnets pull on iron and steel objects.

mcq.q: Which is a source of light energy?
mcq.explain: A torch produces light when it is turned on.

mcq.q: What kind of energy does a moving cyclist have?
mcq.explain: Anything that is moving has kinetic energy — energy of motion.

mcq.q: What does a switch do in a circuit?
choices: Turns the current on or off
mcq.explain: A switch closes or opens the circuit so electricity flows or stops.
Sound is energy that travels in waves through air, water or solids.

mcq.q: Two ends of a magnet are called…
mcq.explain: Magnets have a north pole and a south pole.
Like poles push each other away — they repel.

mcq.q: What happens if you pull a rubber band, then let go?
choices: It snaps back to its old shape
mcq.explain: A rubber band has stored elastic energy that pulls it back.

mcq.q: Which appliance uses electrical energy to make heat?
mcq.explain: An electric iron uses electricity to heat its plate to press clothes.

mcq.q: A push or pull that changes the speed of an object is a…
mcq.explain: A force can speed up, slow down, or change the direction of an object.

mcq.q: Why do we need oil on machine parts?
mcq.explain: Oil makes surfaces slide more easily, which reduces friction and wear.

flashcards.back: Force that pulls things toward Earth
Force that resists motion between two surfaces
Force from magnets that attracts iron
Energy from sources that do not run out (sun, wind)

mcq.explain: Earth orbits the Sun once every year (365.25 days).

mcq.q: How long does Earth take to spin once on its axis?
mcq.explain: Earth rotates once in 24 hours, giving us day and night.

mcq.q: Which is a natural satellite of Earth?
mcq.explain: The Moon is Earth's natural satellite.

mcq.q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
mcq.explain: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.

choices: Light is bent by moving air
mcq.explain: Starlight bends as it passes through moving air in our atmosphere, so it appears to twinkle.

choices: The Earth rotates on its axis
mcq.explain: Earth's rotation causes the side facing the Sun to have day.

mcq.q: How many planets are in our Solar System?
mcq.explain: There are 8 planets in our Solar System: Mercury through Neptune.
The Sun is a giant ball of hot gas — a star.

mcq.q: Which planet do we live on?
mcq.explain: We live on the third planet from the Sun, Earth.

mcq.q: How long does Earth take to go around the Sun once?
mcq.explain: One full orbit around the Sun is one year — about 365 days.

mcq.q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
mcq.explain: Mars looks reddish because of iron-rich rust on its surface.
The Moon is Earth's natural satellite — it orbits the Earth.
The Earth is tilted, so different parts get more sunlight at different times of the year.
Saturn is famous for its bright system of rings made of ice and rock.

choices: A pattern of stars in the sky
mcq.explain: A constellation is a group of stars that people see as a picture or pattern.

mcq.q: Which is the biggest planet in our Solar System?
mcq.explain: Jupiter is the largest planet — bigger than all the others put together.
Rockets are powerful enough to escape Earth's gravity.

mcq.q: What do we call a piece of rock that falls from space and burns in the sky?
mcq.explain: A meteor (shooting star) is a small piece of space rock burning in the atmosphere.

mcq.q: Day happens on the side of Earth that…
mcq.explain: As Earth spins, the side facing the Sun has daylight.
The Sun makes its own light. The Moon only reflects the Sun's light.

flashcards.back: A large body that orbits the Sun
A huge ball of hot glowing gas, like our Sun
The Sun and everything that orbits it
Earth spinning on its axis (24 hours)
Earth travelling around the Sun (1 year)
The path one object takes around another in space

choices: All living and non-living things around us
mcq.explain: The environment is everything around us — air, water, land, living and non-living things.

choices: Using materials again to make new things
mcq.explain: Recycling turns used materials like plastic bottles or paper into new products.

mcq.q: Which is good for the environment?
mcq.explain: Planting trees helps clean the air and gives homes to animals.

mcq.q: Which is a type of pollution?
mcq.explain: Plastic waste in rivers is a form of water pollution.
Tractors are machines that help farmers plough, plant, and harvest.

mcq.q: Which is a renewable energy source?
mcq.explain: Sunlight is renewable — it doesn't run out. Coal and oil will eventually run out.

mcq.q: Which is a renewable energy source?
mcq.explain: Wind keeps blowing — it doesn't run out, so it is renewable.
Trees clean the air and provide homes for animals.
Smoke from burning rubbish pollutes the air.
Petroleum (oil), coal and natural gas are fossil fuels formed long ago.

mcq.q: Which technology helps doctors see inside the body?
mcq.explain: X-ray machines take pictures through skin to show bones and other parts.
These three Rs lower waste and keep our resources from being used up.

mcq.q: Which is harmful to the environment?
mcq.explain: Plastic bags hurt sea animals and last for hundreds of years.
Solar panels capture sunlight and convert it into electrical energy.

mcq.q: The internet is mainly used for…
mcq.explain: The internet connects computers so people can share information and messages.

mcq.q: Which technology helps fishermen find fish?
mcq.explain: Sonar uses sound waves under water to locate schools of fish.

mcq.q: Cutting down too many trees is called…
mcq.explain: Deforestation removes trees that protect soil and clean the air.

mcq.q: Why should plastic be sorted from other rubbish?
mcq.explain: Sorting lets plastic be sent for recycling into new items.

choices: Turns food scraps into fertiliser for soil
mcq.explain: Composting recycles plant scraps into rich soil for gardens.

mcq.q: Which is a healthy environment for fish?
mcq.explain: Clean reefs are full of life and food, perfect for many fish.

flashcards.back: Everything around us — living and non-living
Harmful substances added to air, water, or land
Turn used materials into new things
Use something again instead of throwing it away
Use less to create less waste

mcq.q: A simple machine that helps us lift heavy things using a bar is called a…
mcq.explain: A lever rests on a fulcrum and makes lifting easier — like a seesaw.

mcq.q: A ramp is an example of a…
mcq.explain: A ramp is an inclined plane — a slanted surface that reduces the force needed to lift.
A pulley has a wheel with a rope over it; pulling one end lifts the load.
An axe splits wood — the blade acts as a wedge.

mcq.q: A complex machine is made of…
mcq.explain: Complex machines like a bicycle combine many simple machines.
A screw is an inclined plane wrapped around a rod.

mcq.q: A wheel and axle helps things to…
mcq.explain: A wheel turns on its axle so loads can be moved with less effort.

mcq.q: Which simple machine is a knife?
mcq.explain: A knife's sharp edge is a wedge that splits things apart.

mcq.q: A see-saw is an example of a…
mcq.explain: A see-saw turns on a pivot, which makes it a lever.

mcq.q: A staircase is like which simple machine?
mcq.explain: A staircase lets you reach a high place with less effort, like a ramp (inclined plane).
A pulley uses a rope and wheel to lift loads more easily.

mcq.q: Which simple machine is in a flag pole?
mcq.explain: A pulley at the top lets you raise the flag by pulling down on the rope.

mcq.q: A bicycle is a complex machine because it uses…
mcq.explain: A bicycle uses wheels, levers (pedals) and gears all working together.

mcq.q: A doorknob uses which simple machine?
mcq.explain: Turning the round knob (a wheel) twists the axle to open the latch.

choices: To carry heavy loads with less effort
mcq.explain: A wheelbarrow combines a lever and a wheel to make heavy loads easier to move.

mcq.q: Which is NOT a simple machine?
mcq.explain: A computer is a complex machine made of many simpler parts.

mcq.q: A nail being hammered into wood acts as a…
mcq.explain: A nail's sharp point splits wood apart like a wedge.

mcq.q: A jar lid uses which simple machine?
mcq.explain: The threads on a jar and lid are screws — they hold things tightly.
Each blade is a wedge, and the handles work as levers around the pivot.

mcq.q: Which machine helps a builder lift bricks to a high floor?
mcq.explain: A crane uses pulleys and motors to lift heavy loads safely.

flashcards.back: Bar that lifts loads using a fulcrum
Two inclined planes — splits or cuts
Inclined plane wrapped around a rod
Wheel with a rope for lifting
A wheel on a rod — reduces friction
The point a lever turns on
The object being moved or lifted

mcq.q: Which rock is formed from cooled lava?
mcq.explain: Igneous rocks form when hot lava or magma cools and hardens.

mcq.explain: A cube has 6 square faces.

mcq.q: How many edges does a cube have?
mcq.explain: A cube has 12 edges where its faces meet.

mcq.q: How many vertices (corners) does a cube have?
mcq.explain: A cube has 8 corners (vertices).
A sphere is a perfectly round 3-D shape, like a ball.

mcq.q: Which 3-D shape has a circular base and a point at the top?
mcq.explain: A cone has one circular base and tapers to a point — like an ice-cream cone.

mcq.q: How many sides does an equilateral triangle have that are equal?
mcq.explain: All 3 sides of an equilateral triangle are equal.

mcq.q: An angle smaller than a right angle is called…
mcq.explain: Acute angles are less than 90°.

mcq.q: An angle larger than a right angle but less than 180° is called…
mcq.explain: Obtuse angles are more than 90° but less than 180°.

mcq.q: How many degrees are in a straight angle?
mcq.explain: A straight line forms a 180° angle.

mcq.q: A rectangle has how many right angles?
mcq.explain: All 4 corners of a rectangle are right angles.
A sphere has no flat faces, so it can roll in any direction.

mcq.explain: The pattern adds 2 each time: 8 + 2 = 10.
The pattern adds 5: 20 + 5 = 25.

mcq.q: What comes next? A, C, E, G, ?
mcq.explain: Skip one letter each time: I.
The pattern alternates ▲ and ●, so next is ●.
The pattern adds 3 each time, so next is 15.
The pattern subtracts 10, so next is 60.
These are square numbers (1, 4, 9, 16, 25).
The pattern alternates star and diamond, so next is ◆.
Add 3 each time: 11 + 3 = 14.

mcq.q: What comes next? Z, Y, X, W, ?
mcq.explain: The letters go backwards through the alphabet, so V comes next.
Each number doubles: 8 × 2 = 16.
Add 7 each time: 14 + 7 = 21.

mcq.explain: The mode is the number that appears most often: 4.

mcq.q: The chance of flipping a fair coin and getting heads is…
mcq.explain: A coin has 2 sides — the chance is 1 out of 2, or 1/2.

mcq.q: If it rains every day in June, tomorrow's rain is…
mcq.explain: If it rains every day, tomorrow's rain is very likely.
The median is the middle value when numbers are in order: 7.

mcq.q: You roll a fair 6-sided die. The chance of rolling a 7 is…
mcq.explain: A 6-sided die only has 1–6, so 7 is impossible.
9 appears most often (three times), so it is the mode.
Range = highest − lowest = 18 − 5 = 13.

mcq.q: What is the chance of an event that is certain?
mcq.explain: A certain event has probability 1 (or 100%).

mcq.q: The chance of an impossible event is…
mcq.explain: An impossible event has probability 0.

mcq.q: You roll a fair die. What is the chance of rolling an even number?
mcq.explain: 3 of 6 sides are even (2, 4, 6) — that's 3/6 = 1/2.
The middle number when sorted is 3.
With an even count, the median is the average of the two middle numbers: (4 + 6)/2 = 5.
'Most likely' means highest chance among the options, but not certain.

mcq.q: A bag has 4 red marbles and 1 blue. What is most likely if you pick one without looking?
mcq.explain: There are far more red marbles, so red is most likely.

mcq.q: In a survey, 20 students chose mango and 5 chose orange. The mode is…
mcq.explain: Mango has more votes, so it's the mode (most chosen).

mcq.q: The chance of pulling a king from a fair pack of 52 cards is…
mcq.explain: There are 4 kings in 52 cards, so 4/52.
8 appears the most (three times), so it is the mode.

The number that appears most often

A book is on sale: J$300 instead of J$500. You save…

Half of 20 is 10.

2/4 = 1/2 (half of 4 is 2).

mcq.explain: 1 whole − 1/4 = 3/4 left.
2/3 means more parts than 1/3, so it is bigger.
2/4 = 1/2 because both halves are equal.
The top number of a fraction is the numerator.

mcq.q: Three-quarters of an hour is how many minutes?
mcq.explain: 3/4 × 60 = 45 minutes.
With the same numerator, the smaller the denominator, the larger the fraction.

mcq.q: If you eat 2/8 of a pizza, that is the same as…
mcq.explain: 2/8 = 1/4 (divide both by 2).
3/4 is closest to a whole.

mcq.q: A whole pizza divided into 8 equal slices: 4 slices = ?
mcq.explain: 4/8 = 1/2 of the pizza.
With the same denominator, 3/5 > 2/5.

mcq.explain: 0.5 is the largest (half).

mcq.q: Round 3.7 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: 3.7 is closer to 4 than to 3.

mcq.q: The digit after the decimal point in 2.35 shows…
mcq.explain: The first digit after a decimal point is the tenths place (3 tenths).

0.25 + 0.25 = 0.50, which is the same as 0.5.

mcq.explain: 0.62 is more than 0.5, so round up to 5.

mcq.explain: After the decimal: tenths, then hundredths.
0.05 is just five hundredths — smaller than the others.

mcq.q: Which decimal is the same as 7/10?

mcq.explain: 0.6 means six tenths.

mcq.q: Round 12.4 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: 0.4 is less than 0.5, so round down to 12.

mcq.explain: 100% means the whole, so 100% of 50 = 50.

mcq.explain: 0.5 = 50%, which is more than 25%.

mcq.q: A pie chart is shaped like a…
mcq.explain: A pie chart is a circle cut into slices representing parts of a whole.

mcq.explain: A line graph is best for showing changes across time (e.g. temperature by day).

mcq.explain: The title describes the graph's topic.
Tally marks group in 5s (IIII with a line through).

choices: To explain what the graph is about
mcq.explain: The title tells the reader what the graph is showing.

mcq.q: On a bar graph the bars can be drawn…
mcq.explain: Bars in a bar graph can be vertical or horizontal.

mcq.q: Which graph is good for showing parts of a whole?
mcq.explain: A pie chart shows how parts make up a whole.
In a pictograph, each picture stands for a quantity.

mcq.q: If 5 students like ackee and 3 like callaloo, how many more like ackee?
mcq.explain: 5 − 3 = 2 more.

mcq.q: In a tally, |||| (4 lines) plus a slash means…
mcq.explain: A slash through 4 marks counts as 5.

mcq.q: Which axis of a graph usually shows numbers?
mcq.explain: The vertical (y) axis usually shows numerical values.

mcq.q: In a survey, mango: 12, banana: 8, apple: 4. The mode is…
mcq.explain: Mango has the most votes (12), so it is the mode.

mcq.q: To compare how many of each, the best graph is a…
mcq.explain: A bar graph makes it easy to compare amounts.
5 (with slash) + 3 = 8.

mcq.q: A line graph is best for showing…
mcq.explain: Line graphs show how a value changes through time.

mcq.q: On a graph, the label on the side tells you…
mcq.explain: Axis labels explain what is being measured.

mcq.q: If 2 children chose apple and 5 chose mango, total surveyed?
mcq.explain: 2 + 5 = 7 children.

mcq.q: Which graph is best for showing the number of patties sold each day of the week?
mcq.explain: A bar graph compares amounts across categories like days.

mcq.explain: 3 × 2 = 6 eaten; 8 − 6 = 2 left.

mcq.q: A book has 120 pages. You read 45 pages. How many pages are left?
If 4 friends share 12 mangoes equally, each gets…
A farmer plants 6 rows of 7 yams each. Total yams?

mcq.explain: 8 × 6 = 48 mangoes.

mcq.q: A class has 28 students. 4 are absent. How many present?
Sara reads 15 pages a day. How many pages in 5 days?
A water tank has 100 L. 35 L is used. How much is left?
A pen costs J$60. How many can you buy with J$300?
6 friends share 24 sweets equally. Each gets…
mcq.explain: 24 ÷ 6 = 4 sweets each.

mcq.q: A train leaves at 9:00 and arrives at 11:30. The trip takes…
mcq.explain: From 9:00 to 11:30 is 2 hours 30 minutes.

mcq.q: A rectangle is 6 cm long and 4 cm wide. Its perimeter is…
mcq.explain: Perimeter = 2(L + W) = 2(6 + 4) = 20 cm.

mcq.q: A square has sides of 5 cm. Its area is…
mcq.explain: Area of a square = side × side = 5 × 5 = 25 cm².

mcq.q: You have J$2,000 and a toy costs J$1,750. Can you afford it?
mcq.explain: 2,000 is more than 1,750, so yes.

mcq.q: A book has 200 pages. You read 1/4. How many pages did you read?
mcq.explain: 1/4 of 200 = 50 pages.

mcq.q: A jar holds 500 mL. You pour 250 mL into a glass. How much remains in the jar?

choices: Paying attention and thinking about what is said
mcq.explain: Active listening means focusing fully on the speaker.

mcq.q: When presenting in class, you should…
choices: Speak clearly and make eye contact
mcq.explain: Clear speech and eye contact help your audience understand you.

mcq.q: What should you do before answering a question?
mcq.explain: Take a moment to think so your answer makes sense.

choices: Both talking and listening in turn
mcq.explain: Good conversation requires both speaking and listening.

mcq.q: If you don't understand something, you should…
mcq.explain: Asking questions shows good listening and helps learning.

choices: The feeling in how you speak
mcq.explain: Your tone expresses how you feel — happy, angry, excited.
Eye contact shows the speaker you are paying attention.

mcq.q: When you are not sure what someone said you can ask…
mcq.explain: Asking politely for a repeat is a good listening habit.

choices: Saying each word so it can be heard
mcq.explain: Clear speech helps your listener understand you.

choices: Eye contact and a friendly tone
mcq.explain: Eye contact and a friendly tone keep listeners engaged.

mcq.q: When someone is speaking, you should NOT…
mcq.explain: Interrupting is rude and breaks the speaker's thought.

mcq.q: To stay on topic in a conversation means…
choices: Stick to what is being discussed
mcq.explain: Keeping to the topic helps everyone follow the discussion.

mcq.q: In a debate, both sides should…
mcq.explain: Taking turns lets both sides be heard properly.

mcq.q: A 'turn-taking' rule helps a class because…
mcq.explain: Taking turns gives everyone a chance to speak.
Body language is what your face and body show without words.
Short clear steps make directions easy to follow.

mcq.q: A polite way to disagree is to say…
choices: I see your point, but I think…
mcq.explain: Polite phrases let you share a different view without upsetting people.

mcq.q: Speaking too quickly often makes you…
mcq.explain: Going too fast makes words run together so listeners can't follow.
An audience is the group listening to a speaker or performer.
Active listeners show they are paying attention by responding.

mcq.q: The main idea of a story is…
choices: What the story is mostly about
mcq.explain: The main idea is the central point the author wants to share.

choices: A person or animal in the story
mcq.explain: Characters are the people or creatures in the story.

choices: Where and when the story takes place
mcq.explain: The setting is the time and place of the story.

choices: Something that can be proven true
mcq.explain: A fact is information that can be checked and proven.
An opinion is a personal view — not provable as fact.
Inferring is reading between the lines using clues in the text.

choices: A short retelling of the main points
A long copy of the story
mcq.explain: A summary captures the main ideas in a few sentences.
Readers predict by using clues to guess what comes next.
The plot is the sequence of events in the story.
Point of view is the perspective from which the story is told.
Facts can be checked and proven true.
An opinion is what someone thinks or believes.

mcq.q: To skim a text means to…
choices: Read quickly to get the main idea
mcq.explain: Skimming is reading fast to get the gist.
A glossary is a small dictionary in the back of the book.

choices: Words around an unknown word that help guess its meaning
mcq.explain: Words and sentences nearby give clues about the meaning of new words.

mcq.q: Re-reading a part of the text helps you…
mcq.explain: Re-reading helps you catch details you may have missed.

choices: A cause makes something happen and the effect is the result
mcq.explain: Cause is what made it happen; effect is what happened.
Nonfiction gives true information about real people, places or events.
Fiction is imagined or invented by the author.

choices: Find what is the same and different
mcq.explain: Comparing looks at both similarities and differences.

flashcards.back: The central point of a text
A person or animal in a story
Where and when a story happens
The events that happen in a story

mcq.explain: Every sentence needs a subject (who/what) and a verb (action).
'The boy ran quickly.' has a subject (boy) and verb (ran).
Sentences begin with capital letters.
Statements end with full stops.
Questions end with a question mark.

mcq.q: Which type of sentence is: 'Close the door.'?
mcq.explain: A command tells someone to do something.

mcq.q: A noun phrase always has a…
mcq.explain: A noun phrase is a noun and the words around it.
A question asks for information; it would end with '?'.
Commands give an instruction, like 'Close the door.'

mcq.q: A sentence that shows strong feeling ends with a…
mcq.explain: Exclamation marks (!) show strong feeling.

mcq.q: Which is the subject in: 'Maya kicked the ball.'?
mcq.explain: The subject is who or what does the action — Maya.

mcq.q: Which is the verb in: 'The dog barked loudly.'?
mcq.explain: 'Barked' is the action — the verb.
'Running fast.' has no subject, so it's a fragment.

choices: A sentence that runs out of paper
Two sentences joined without proper punctuation
mcq.explain: Run-ons join ideas wrongly and need punctuation or a conjunction.
'But' joins two clauses or ideas.

mcq.q: Which is the predicate in: 'My sister sings every morning.'?
mcq.explain: The predicate tells what the subject does — 'sings every morning'.

mcq.q: Which sentence has a compound subject?
mcq.explain: A compound subject has two or more subjects joined by 'and'.

mcq.q: In 'She is reading a book', 'is reading' is the…
mcq.explain: 'Is reading' is a verb made of more than one word — a verb phrase.

mcq.q: Which type of sentence is: 'The sea is blue.'?
mcq.explain: It states a fact, so it's a statement.

mcq.q: Which type of sentence is: 'How beautiful the sunset is!'?
mcq.explain: Strong feeling and an exclamation mark make it an exclamation.

flashcards.back: Group of words with a subject and verb
Who or what the sentence is about
A sentence that tells you to do something

mcq.q: A paragraph is a group of sentences about…
mcq.explain: Good paragraphs focus on one topic/idea.
Planning helps you organise thoughts and stay on topic.
Stories have beginning, middle, and end.
Editing means reviewing and improving your writing.

choices: Tells the main idea of a paragraph
mcq.explain: A topic sentence introduces the paragraph's main idea.
Descriptive writing paints pictures using sensory language.
A draft is your first try; you can improve it later.
Brainstorming gathers ideas before you start writing.

mcq.q: A good story has a beginning, middle, and…
mcq.explain: Stories follow a beginning–middle–end structure.
Proofreading catches errors before publishing.

choices: At the start of a paragraph
mcq.explain: The topic sentence introduces the paragraph's main idea.
All sentences in a paragraph should support the same idea.
Descriptive writing uses words that help readers see and feel.

choices: Asks the reader to be quiet
mcq.explain: Persuasive writing argues for a point of view.

mcq.q: A friendly letter usually starts with…
mcq.explain: Friendly letters open with 'Dear' and the person's name.

mcq.q: In a letter, the closing is…
mcq.explain: The closing is your sign-off before your name.

mcq.q: To organise ideas you can use a…
mcq.explain: A graphic organiser, like a web or chart, helps plan your writing.
Editing makes the writing clearer and more correct.
A conclusion ends the piece and ties ideas together.

choices: To organise ideas and make reading easier
mcq.explain: Paragraphs separate ideas so the reader can follow.

flashcards.back: Group of sentences on one idea
The first version of your writing
Writing that explains or gives information

mcq.explain: Joyful means the same as happy.
An antonym is the opposite; small is the opposite of big.
Remember: 'i' before 'e' except after 'c'.
Homophones sound the same but have different meanings.
Enormous means extremely large.
Friend: 'i' before 'e'.
'Large' has a similar meaning to 'big'.
'Sad' is the opposite of 'happy'.
The rule is 'i before e except after c' — receive.
Homophones sound the same but mean different things, like 'sea' and 'see'.
'Gigantic' means extremely big.
The correct spelling is necessary.
'Quick' means the same as 'fast'.
'Cold' is the opposite of 'hot'.
The correct spelling is beautiful.
To mend is to fix or repair something.
'Furious' means very angry.

mcq.explain: 'Pair' means two of something — and is a homophone of 'pear'.

'Rare' means not common — hardly ever found.

mcq.explain: Commas separate items in lists and show pauses.

mcq.q: Which punctuation ends an exciting sentence?

mcq.explain: 'We' starts the sentence and 'Paris' is a proper noun — both need capitals.

mcq.q: A comma can be used to…
mcq.explain: Commas separate items: apples, oranges, and bananas.
"" goes around the words a person speaks.
'Dog's' shows the bone belongs to the dog.

choices: my friend ann lives in kingston.
My friend Ann lives in Kingston.
My friend ann lives in Kingston.
my friend Ann lives in kingston.
mcq.explain: Sentences and proper nouns (Ann, Kingston) need capitals.
A question mark ends a question.

mcq.q: Which mark ends an exciting sentence?
mcq.explain: Exclamation marks show strong feeling.
Capitals for the title and name; full stop ends the sentence.
Commas go between items in a list.

mcq.q: A colon (:) can be used to…
mcq.explain: A colon often introduces a list or example.

mcq.q: Which day name is capitalised correctly?
mcq.explain: Days of the week start with a capital letter.

mcq.q: In 'I'm happy', the apostrophe stands for…
mcq.explain: I'm is a contraction of 'I am' — the apostrophe replaces 'a'.

choices: I bought rice beans and yams.
I bought rice, beans, and yams.
I bought, rice and beans, and yams,
mcq.explain: Commas separate the items in the list.

flashcards.back: Used at the start of sentences and for names
, — a short pause or list separator

mcq.explain: The theme is the underlying message (like 'kindness wins').
A simile compares using 'like' or 'as' (brave as a lion).
A metaphor says one thing IS another (e.g., 'her eyes were stars').

choices: The character who opposes the hero
mcq.explain: The antagonist is the character who creates problems for the hero.
Dialogue is what characters say to each other.

mcq.q: The climax of a story is…
mcq.explain: The climax is the turning point — the most exciting part.

mcq.q: A simile compares two things using…
mcq.explain: A simile uses 'like' or 'as' (e.g., 'as fast as a cheetah').
A metaphor says one thing is another (e.g., 'time is money').

Personification gives human traits to non-human things, like 'the wind whispered'.

choices: When and where the story happens
mcq.explain: Setting is the time and place.
Conflict is the problem or struggle that drives the story.
Resolution is when the conflict is sorted out.

choices: Words sound like what they mean
mcq.explain: Examples: 'buzz', 'splash', 'crash'.

choices: Feeling the story gives the reader
mcq.explain: Mood is the emotional atmosphere of the story.

mcq.q: In a fable, the moral is…
mcq.explain: Fables end with a moral lesson, like 'slow and steady wins the race'.

mcq.explain: A poem is divided into stanzas like a story is divided into paragraphs.

choices: Words start with the same sound
mcq.explain: Example: 'Sally sells seashells'.

choices: A story passed down through generations
mcq.explain: Folktales are traditional stories of a culture, often with morals.

mcq.q: When should you use Standard English?

mcq.explain: Use Standard English for formal settings like school and exams.

choices: Changing language to suit the situation
mcq.explain: Code-switching means shifting between Patois and Standard English depending on setting.
Patois has its own grammar and patterns — it is a full language.

mcq.q: Standard English in Jamaica is used for…
mcq.explain: Standard English is used in school, work and official settings.

choices: A unique language with its own rules
mcq.explain: Patois is a real language with its own grammar — not bad English.

mcq.q: Standard English for 'Mi waan one patty' is…
mcq.explain: Standard English uses 'I' as the subject and 'a' before consonant sounds.

choices: Switching between Standard English and Creole
mcq.explain: Code-switching lets you choose the right language for the situation.
'Good morning, how are you?' is the most formal greeting.
Patois is most often heard in everyday talk and Jamaican culture.

mcq.q: Standard English for 'mi nuh know' is…
mcq.explain: Standard English uses 'I do not know' or its contraction 'I don't know'.

mcq.q: Which job uses Standard English most?
mcq.explain: News broadcasts use Standard English so most listeners understand.

mcq.q: Standard English for 'Wi a go a beach' is…
choices: We are going to the beach.
mcq.explain: Standard English uses 'are going' and 'to the' before the place.

mcq.q: Knowing both Patois and Standard English makes you…
mcq.explain: Knowing both gives you more ways to communicate — a strength.
Standard English uses the helper verb 'has gone'.
'Wha gwaan?' is a friendly 'What's happening?'

mcq.q: Standard English for 'Mi belly full' is…
mcq.explain: In Standard English we say 'I am full.'

mcq.q: In a school essay you should write in…
mcq.explain: School essays are written in Standard English.

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Standard English & Creole

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Standard English & Creole

mcq.q: A noun is a word for…
choices: A person, place, thing, or idea
mcq.explain: Nouns name people, places, things, or ideas.
Proper nouns name specific things and start with capital letters.
'She' replaces a female name.
Books is plural — more than one book.
'They' replaces groups of people or things.

mcq.q: Which noun is abstract (cannot be touched)?
mcq.explain: Abstract nouns like love, happiness, or fear cannot be touched.
'Boy' is a general name — a common noun.
Specific names like 'Rio Grande' are proper nouns and start with capitals.
For words ending in y after a consonant, change y to i and add es.
'Feet' is an irregular plural.

mcq.q: The pronoun for 'a group of girls' is…
mcq.explain: 'They' is used for groups of people.
'Mine' shows that something belongs to me.

mcq.q: In 'The cat caught the mouse', the noun(s) are…
mcq.explain: 'Cat' and 'mouse' are both nouns (things).
A collective noun names a group, like 'team'.
'Children' is the irregular plural of 'child'.
Abstract nouns name things you cannot touch, like 'love'.
'Their' shows possession — the keys belong to them.

mcq.q: What is the pronoun for 'Mr and Mrs Brown'?
mcq.explain: Two people together are 'they'.
'Child' is singular; 'children' is the plural.

mcq.q: Replace the underlined nouns with a pronoun: 'Tomi and I went home.'
mcq.explain: Tomi and I together is 'we'.

flashcards.back: Word that replaces a noun (he, she, it)

mcq.explain: Verbs show action (run) or state (is, am, are).
Run becomes 'ran' in simple past tense.

mcq.q: Which sentence is in the future tense?
mcq.explain: 'Will play' shows something will happen later.

mcq.q: Choose the correct verb: 'She ___ to school every day.'
mcq.explain: With 'she' in present tense, use 'goes'.
'-ing' verbs show ongoing (continuous) action.

mcq.q: What is the past tense of 'write'?
mcq.explain: 'Wrote' is the irregular past tense of write.

mcq.q: A verb is a word that shows…
mcq.explain: Verbs show what someone or something does or is.
'Went' is the past tense of 'go'.
'Saw' is the simple past tense of 'see'.

mcq.q: Which sentence is in present tense?
mcq.explain: 'Walks' is the present-tense verb.

mcq.q: Which sentence is in future tense?
mcq.explain: 'Will play' is the future tense.
'Ate' is the simple past tense of 'eat'.

mcq.q: Choose the correct verb: 'They ___ in the park yesterday.'
mcq.explain: 'Yesterday' shows past tense — 'played'.

mcq.q: Choose the correct verb: 'She ___ to school every day.'
mcq.explain: With 'she' (singular), the present tense is 'goes'.
'Ran' is the simple past tense of 'run'.
'Is' helps the main verb (e.g., 'is running').

mcq.q: The verb in 'The bird sings sweetly' is…
mcq.explain: 'Sings' is the action verb.
'Wrote' is the past tense of 'write'.

mcq.q: Choose the correct verb: 'The dog ___ loudly.'
mcq.explain: With 'dog' (singular, present), use 'barks'.

mcq.q: Which form of the verb is 'eating'?
mcq.explain: Verbs ending in -ing are present participles.

flashcards.back: Goes with main verb (is, has, will)

mcq.explain: Adjectives describe nouns (the blue sky).

mcq.q: In 'the tall boy', the adjective is…
mcq.explain: 'Tall' describes 'boy'.
Most adverbs describe verbs — how, when, or where the action happens.

mcq.q: In 'she runs quickly', the adverb is…
mcq.explain: 'Quickly' tells HOW she runs.
Many adverbs end with -ly (slowly, loudly).
Happy describes a noun (a happy child).

mcq.q: In 'a sweet mango', the adjective is…
mcq.explain: 'Sweet' describes the mango — it's the adjective.

mcq.q: In 'she sings beautifully', the adverb is…
mcq.explain: 'Beautifully' describes how she sings — it's an adverb.
Most adverbs end in -ly, like 'quickly'.
'Brave' describes a noun, so it's an adjective.
Adjectives describe nouns (things, people, places).
Adverbs describe verbs — telling how, when or where.

mcq.q: Choose the adjective: 'The angry dog barked.'
mcq.explain: 'Angry' describes the dog.
'Loudly' describes how he laughed.
Numbers like 'three' tell how many.
'Today' tells when something happens.
'Outside' tells where something happens.

mcq.q: In 'a tall, kind teacher', how many adjectives?
mcq.explain: 'Tall' and 'kind' both describe teacher — 2 adjectives.
Add -er for the comparative form.
Add -est for the superlative form.

choices: Added to the START of a word
mcq.explain: Prefixes attach to the beginning (un-, re-, pre-).
Suffixes attach to the end (-ing, -ed, -ly).
The prefix 'un-' means 'not'.
'-er' means someone who does the action (a teacher teaches).

choices: The main part of the word
mcq.explain: The root carries the core meaning (e.g., 'teach' in teacher).
'Re-' means again (rewrite = write again).
'Re-' often means again, as in 'rewrite'.
'Un-' means not, as in 'unhappy'.
'-ful' means full of, as in 'helpful'.
'-less' means without, as in 'careless'.
'Pre-' means before, like a 'preview' before the show.
'Kind' is the root; 'un-' and '-ly' are added.
-ful means full of, so colourful = full of colour.
-less means without, so fearless = without fear.
'-er' often makes a noun for someone who does something.
'Re-' means again, so rebuild = build again.
'Happy' is the root; '-ness' is added (the y becomes i).
'Mis-' means wrong or badly, as in 'misunderstand'.
'Un-' means not, so unfair = not fair.
'-able' means can be, as in 'readable' = can be read.
